what_it_does: Implements a durable corpus-record layer: it validates required session/round/project lineage, writes immutable content-addressed records under deterministic run:// pointers, indexes them as corpus.record events on the store's event log, reads them back, projects/lists/finds them, and provides a deterministic repo file walker for ingestion.
observations: Lineage is a fail-loud gate: _validate_lineage refuses any record missing session/round/project before writing.
The module deliberately distinguishes three lineage axes: corpus lineage (session/round/project), store provenance-lineage, and decision-lineage.
Indexing uses a distinct durable event kind corpus.record, not op.run, to avoid polluting the closed engine-run grammar.
list_corpus and find_corpus are read-time projections over store.events_since(-1), with dedup-on-read by address using latest seq.
write_record omits a timestamp from the content record so identical re-writes hash to the same CAS, making resume idempotent.
It is explicitly a thin wrapper over store.put_content / set_ref / append_event / get_content / head / events_since; it does not edit fs_store or vector_index.
walk_files is a deterministic, sorted, hidden-directory-skipping file walker with normalized paths and per-file head slicing.
source_address is guarded against being passed as content (length > 200 or newlines) to avoid filename-too-long errors.
Record schema is open/additive: extra fields ride free, but required fields are enforced.
Constants REQUIRED_RECORD_FIELDS, LINEAGE_FIELDS, CORPUS_EVENT_KIND, WALK_* are declared at module level.
standouts: The lineage gate: required session/round/project, fail-loud, no defaults.
Three separate 'lineage' axes are explicitly named and disambiguated in the docstring.
Event-log-as-index with a dedicated corpus.record kind, mirroring the run-index pattern but staying separate from engine-run ops.
Deterministic repo walk with hidden-dir skipping and path normalization, exposed as a module-level utility.
conventions: Fail-loud validation with explicit CorpusError and guidance messages.
Reuse-don't-parallel: only existing store public methods, no new DB or fs_store edits.
Immutable CAS content plus mutable run:// pointer for resume-safe idempotency.
Schema-additive open records: required fields enforced, unknown fields allowed.
Heavy docstring commentary explaining design rationale and cross-references.
Constants for required field sets and event kinds at module top.
concerns: walk_files reads the entire file into memory before slicing the head, which can be expensive for large files.
Skipping every directory starting with '.' may discard legitimate source directories such as .github or .vscode.
list_corpus scans all events since the beginning; no pagination or indexing is provided for large logs.
The 200-character source_address limit and newline check are heuristic and could reject legitimate long addresses.
walk_files opens files without a with statement; while CPython usually closes quickly, it is not idiomatic.
No unit tests or usage examples are present in the file itself.
notes: A lineage-gated, event-log-indexed corpus record wrapper over an existing store, plus a deterministic repo walker.
questions: What exact interface must the store object provide?
Who assigns session, round, and project values at capture time?
How and where does the promised embed-on-write step happen?
What defines a projection and how is it chosen per capture run?
What consumes corpus records for corroboration (M3) and inversion-finding (L2)?
How is resume orchestrated at the caller level?
What are the concrete semantics of store.events_since(-1) and event seq ordering?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a thin corpus-record module that enforces lineage, persists records via the store, indexes them by events, and provides a deterministic file walk.
apparent_themes: Lineage as a first-class, non-optional sequencing gate.
Event log as the single source of truth for indexing.
Immutability + mutable pointers for safe resume and idempotency.
Deterministic ingestion to avoid duplicate identities.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give capture-run outputs a durable, addressed, lineage-tagged home inside an existing store so they can be resumed, queried, and corroborated across sessions without creating a parallel database.
novelty: A required three-axis lineage gate at write time, a dedicated corpus.record event kind separate from the closed op.run grammar, and a promoted deterministic repo walker with hidden-directory skipping and path normalization.
contribution: A minimal, lineage-enforced corpus-record contract and a deterministic file walker that let capture runs persist and query addressed records on top of an existing content store and event log.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/corpus.py defines the corpus-record layer: lineage validation, immutable content-addressed record writes under deterministic run:// pointers, corpus.record event-log indexing, read/list/find projections, and a deterministic repo file walker.
intention_for_embedding: To make capture-run outputs durable, addressed, and cross-session traceable while reusing the existing store and event log instead of adding a new database.
suggested_fields: source_hash (mentioned as optional extra but not a required/standard record field)
embedding_cas (to link the separately embedded representation)
capture_run_status (to track whether a run is complete, resumed, or failed)
proposed_edge_kinds: resume-overwrites (later record event -> earlier record event at same address)

symbols.does: Exception class for corpus-record contract violations.
symbols.description: Subclass of ValueError raised when a record lacks required fields, malformed lineage, or an invalid source_address; implements the fail-loud D1 contract.

symbols.does: Builds the deterministic run:// address for a corpus record.
symbols.description: Combines project, source_address, and optional projection into a stable run://corpus/... pointer so re-runs overwrite the same logical address and different projections get distinct records.

symbols.does: Fails loud if lineage is missing any required axis.
symbols.description: Checks that lineage is a dict containing truthy session, round, and project values, normalizes them to strings, and returns the validated dict; refuses incomplete records before any write.

symbols.does: Persists one validated corpus record and its index event.
symbols.description: Validates inputs and lineage, computes the deterministic address, writes immutable content to CAS, sets the run:// pointer, and appends a corpus.record event; returns the event augmented with cas and address.

symbols.does: Reads a corpus record by its run:// address.
symbols.description: Resolves the address head to a CAS and returns the stored record dict, or None if the address has never been written.

symbols.does: Projects all corpus records from the event log.
symbols.description: Scans store.events_since(-1), filters corpus.record events, optionally filters by project, deduplicates by address keeping the latest seq, and returns rows newest-first.

symbols.does: Filters corpus records by kind, projection, and source_address.
symbols.description: Builds on list_corpus and applies optional equality filters for record kind, projection lens, and source_address.

symbols.does: Deterministically walks repo roots and returns file head slices.
symbols.description: Recursively walks given roots in sorted order, skips hidden and junk directories and non-matching extensions, reads UTF-8 text, drops short files, normalizes paths, and returns a list of {path, text} dicts with a per-file character cap.
